expectations.text: Acknowledges the saturated/lazy-contrarian framing the user explicitly worries about
expectations.evidence: Multiple direct references: 'this is the move that separates you from the lazy contrarians', 'the line between thoughtful re-examination and smug contrarian is whether you implicate yourself in Beat 2'.

expectations.text: Picks a structure that includes setbacks or earned credibility rather than pure optimistic arc
expectations.evidence: Explicitly chooses No Easy Way and explains the reasoning: 'Not Man-in-a-Hole (too clean), not Rags-to-Riches (too triumphant)... your audience trusts a story with scars.'

expectations.text: Produces a beat-by-beat talk outline with time estimates or sequence (not just bullet themes)
expectations.evidence: Five labeled beats (1.5min/3min/4min/4min/2.5min) each with content, purpose, and connection to a named tactic.

expectations.text: Plans specifics (numbers, before/after, named incidents) rather than abstract claims
expectations.evidence: Calls out exactly where specifics are needed (Beat 3 setback, Beat 4 metric), provides candidate shapes but flags them as placeholders for the user's real specifics.

expectations.text: Ends with a reframing question or open challenge to the audience rather than a clean wrap
expectations.evidence: Beat 5 is explicitly designed as 'three questions they walk out and run on their own org', closing line is 'whatever you're running right now — make sure you didn't pick it for the same reasons we did.'

expectations.text: Names which tactics or structural devices are being used and why
expectations.evidence: Tactic stack listed at top with per-tactic rationale, each beat has a 'Why this beat is here' note, summary tactics block at the bottom.

summary: 6/6 — disciplined application of named tactics with explicit rationale at every step.
